skill.name: medication-reconciliation
skill.description: Compare patient pre-admission medication lists with inpatient orders to automatically identify omitted or duplicated medications and improve medication safety.
skill.summary: Automated comparison of pre-admission and inpatient medication lists to detect omissions, duplicates, and dose changes.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 15 HIGH severity findings (weak cryptographic algorithm) are false positives caused by the scanner confusing medical drug terminology with cryptographic names. The 20 MEDIUM findings (shell backtick execution) are also false positives from bash code blocks in markdown documentation. The 3 filesystem operations are legitimate data writing for the tool's intended purpose. No malicious patterns, data exfiltration, or prompt injection detected. The skill is well-documented with appropriate medical disclaimers and PHI handling instructions.

medium_findings.title: File Write Operations in Script
medium_findings.description: Python script writes example data and reconciliation reports to disk. These are expected operations for the skill's intended functionality. The --example flag creates test data files, and --output writes the reconciliation report. No access to sensitive system paths.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Source URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded GitHub repository URL for source attribution. This is a legitimate reference to the project source and not a data exfiltration endpoint.

content.user_title: Reconcile Patient Medications Across Care Transitions
content.value_statement: Medication errors during care transitions can lead to serious patient harm. This skill automates the comparison of pre-admission medication lists against inpatient orders to identify omissions, duplicates, and dose changes.
actual_capabilities: Compare pre-admission medication lists against inpatient orders to find discrepancies
Identify continued, discontinued, and newly added medications automatically
Detect dose changes between lists with physician verification warnings
Flag duplicate medications across brand and generic drug names
Generate structured reconciliation reports with clinical recommendations
Alert on missing critical drug classes such as anticoagulants and antiepileptics
limitations: Does not interpret the clinical appropriateness of medication changes
Cannot access live pharmacy systems or electronic health record databases
Requires structured JSON input files for medication list comparison
Does not replace pharmacist or physician medication review and sign-off

use_cases.title: Hospital Admission Medication Review
use_cases.description: Quickly compare a patient's home medications against hospital admission orders to identify discrepancies and prevent medication errors during the admission process.

use_cases.title: Clinical Handover Reconciliation Report
use_cases.description: Generate a structured medication reconciliation report for shift handover to ensure continuity of care and prevent omission of critical medications.

use_cases.title: Medication Safety Audit
use_cases.description: Audit medication reconciliation compliance by comparing documented pre-admission medication lists against inpatient order sets across a patient cohort.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Medication Reconciliation
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the pre-admission medication list in pre_admission.json with the inpatient orders in orders.json. Show me which medications are continued, discontinued, and newly added.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user running a standard reconciliation

prompt_templates.title: Critical Drug Safety Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run medication reconciliation on these files and specifically highlight any missing critical medications such as anticoagulants, hypoglycemics, antihypertensives, or antiepileptics.

prompt_templates.title: Dose Change Detection with Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Reconcile these medication lists and identify any drugs that appear in both lists but with different dosages. For each dose change, include a warning to verify with the prescribing physician.

prompt_templates.title: Full Audit with Recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete reconciliation report with sections for continued medications, dose changes, discontinued drugs, new medications, and duplicates. Include clinical recommendations and flag all critical drug warnings.

output_examples.input: Pre-admission list with Metformin 500mg three times daily. Inpatient order with Metformin 1000mg once daily.
output_examples.output: System flags a dose change for Metformin: pre-admission dose 500mg, inpatient dose 1000mg. Includes warning to verify with prescribing physician before proceeding.

output_examples.input: Pre-admission list with Warfarin 5mg daily and Atorvastatin 20mg nightly. Inpatient orders contain Atorvastatin 20mg but no Warfarin.
output_examples.output: System identifies Atorvastatin as continued. Generates a critical warning that Warfarin (anticoagulant) may be missing and recommends confirming if intentionally discontinued.

output_examples.input: Pre-admission list with Aspirin 100mg daily and Norvasc 5mg daily. Inpatient orders with Bayer Aspirin 100mg daily, Norvasc 5mg daily, and Normal Saline IV.
output_examples.output: System matches Aspirin to Bayer Aspirin via synonym mapping. Lists Normal Saline as a new medication. No critical warnings generated. Reconciliation complete.

best_practices: Confirm patient data has been de-identified before processing to ensure compliance with privacy regulations such as HIPAA.
Review all dose change flags and critical drug warnings with a prescribing physician before updating medication orders.
Include both generic and brand names in medication input files for the most accurate matching results.
anti_patterns: Do not use this tool to make autonomous clinical decisions. It is designed to flag discrepancies for human review only.
Do not rely solely on automated matching for complex drug regimens or polypharmacy cases without manual pharmacist verification.
Do not process incomplete or malformed input files without first correcting the data format and ensuring patient IDs match.

faq.question: What types of medication discrepancies can this tool detect?
faq.answer: It detects omitted medications, duplicate entries, dose changes, newly added medications, and missing critical drug classes.

faq.question: Does this tool connect to electronic health record systems?
faq.answer: No. It requires exported JSON files and does not connect to live EHR systems, pharmacy databases, or any network services.

faq.question: How does the tool handle brand versus generic drug names?
faq.answer: It uses a built-in synonym mapping to match common brand and generic name pairs such as Lipitor and Atorvastatin.

faq.question: Can I customize the list of critical drug classes?
faq.answer: The default list is hardcoded for anticoagulants, hypoglycemics, antihypertensives, and antiepileptics. Future versions may support configuration files.

faq.question: What should I do when a dose change is detected?
faq.answer: Verify the correct dose with the prescribing physician before updating medication orders. The tool cannot determine which dose is clinically appropriate.

faq.question: Is patient data stored or transmitted by this tool?
faq.answer: No. All processing is done locally on the provided input files. Confirm data is de-identified before use.
